preamble: The diphthong /ui/, characterized by a high back rounded vowel followed by a high front unrounded vowel, can undergo a phonetic transformation to /ei/, where the initial element shifts to a mid front unrounded vowel. This change involves a combination of fronting, lowering, and unrounding of the first vowel in the diphthong.
phoneticEffects: Fronting, Lowering, Unrounding of the initial vowel in the diphthong

examples.note: The Standard Mandarin diphthong /ui/ in words like 'duì' (对, meaning 'correct') is pronounced as /ei/ in the Henan dialect.

sources: "Fangyan Friday #13: Historic Henanese - The World of Chinese". (May 15, 2020).
Edkins, J. (1868). A Grammar of Mandarin Dialect. Presbyterian Mission Press.
